title: Counting and Natural Numbers
summary: Counting assigns stable ordered quantities to discrete collections.
claim: Discrete quantities can be represented consistently using ordered symbolic quantities called natural numbers.
proof: Counting follows from one-to-one correspondence and stable successor relationships: after one object is matched to one count marker, the next object advances the count by one.
validation: Level 5 — Mathematical / Logical Proof within arithmetic foundations.
reconstruction: Can be rediscovered by matching stones, marks, or other distinguishable objects one at a time.
